insight.headline: Customer API Changes
insight.what_changed: The Customer Account API has removed the deprecated Customer.lastIncompleteCheckout field and the related Checkout type subtree in version 2026-10. This change affects apps using the Customer Account API to retrieve customer checkout information.
insight.developer_impact: Developers should update their apps to no longer rely on the removed fields. Instead, use alternative fields or APIs to retrieve the necessary customer checkout information. This change may require updates to GraphQL queries, especially those using the Customer Account API. Developers should review their code and test their apps to ensure compatibility with the updated API.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants may notice changes in how customer checkout information is displayed or used within their Shopify stores, depending on the apps they have installed.
insight.why_it_matters: This change helps maintain a clean and efficient API, removing deprecated fields that may cause issues or confusion for developers and merchants.

also_worth_knowing.text: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI now supports non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts during upgrades.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: New fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type, including productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components, starting with API version 2026-07.
CLI version 4.5.1 has been released with updates to cli-hydrogen.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI now reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during deployment.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: Create Hydrogen now adds an explicit ~ app alias to new projects.

updates.title: skeleton@2026.4.5
updates.summary: Hydrogen skeleton now adds an explicit ~ app alias to new projects.

updates.title: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target
updates.summary: POS Extensions now support a background extension target, allowing for event observation and background logic.

updates.title: Identity verification for Shopify Partners starts today
updates.summary: Identity verification for Shopify Partners is now available, enhancing trust in the partner ecosystem.

updates.title: App Pricing: more plans, no-charge plan testing, and negative and fractional App Events
updates.summary: App Pricing has been updated with increased plan limits, no-charge plan testing, and support for negative and fractional App Events.

recap.headline: Hydrogen updates and Shopify API enhancements dominate the week

themes.title: Hydrogen Updates
themes.detail: Multiple Hydrogen updates were released, including @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2, which adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38, which adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects. These updates aim to improve the development experience and fix existing issues.

themes.title: Shopify API Enhancements
themes.detail: New fields were added to the ExchangeLineItem type, including productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle. Additionally, POS UI Extensions now support discount allocations to bundle components and a background extension target, allowing for more flexible and powerful app development.

themes.title: Ecosystem and Policy Updates
themes.detail: Shopify introduced new App Store requirements, including the use of unique app names, and updated App Pricing to include more plans, no-charge plan testing, and support for negative and fractional App Events. Identity verification for Shopify Partners also began, emphasizing trust and security within the ecosystem.

actions.text: Review and update apps to comply with new App Store requirements
Test and implement new Hydrogen features and updates
Explore new possibilities with POS UI Extensions and background extension targets
